description: Biogen is a Cambridge, Massachusetts biotechnology company developing and commercializing therapies for neurological, rare-disease, and immunology indications.

overview.title: Business Focus
paragraphs: Biogen is built around high-barrier therapeutics rather than broad consumer health products: biologics, antisense medicines, specialty neurology drugs, rare-disease programs, and immunology candidates. Its center of gravity remains neurology, but recent strategy emphasizes a more diversified mix of neurology, immunology, and rare disease.

overview.title: Product Footprint
paragraphs: The repo seed products are strong anchors. Spinraza is Biogen's nusinersen therapy for spinal muscular atrophy, while Tysabri is natalizumab for relapsing forms of multiple sclerosis, complete with the kind of serious safety program that reminds everyone biotechnology is not exactly a casual subscription app.

overview.title: Portfolio Direction
paragraphs: Beyond Spinraza and Tysabri, Biogen's current commercial and strategic story includes multiple sclerosis medicines, Leqembi through its Eisai collaboration, Skyclarys, Qalsody, Zurzuvae, biosimilars, and a late-stage pipeline spanning lupus, kidney disease, SMA, Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, and rare neurological disorders.

overview.title: Registry Relevance
paragraphs: No technology wave is mapped yet in the repo context. For Free The World, Biogen should likely be evaluated through the lens of proprietary therapeutics, clinical-trial transparency, patient access, regulatory lock-in, payer economics, and open science substitutes rather than a direct open-source product replacement.
